tluaTableShorthandRemoved.tlua(6,28): error TLUA2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and '1' does not exist in type '{ x: number; }'.
tluaTableShorthandRemoved.tlua(13,2): error TLUA2364: The left-hand side of an assignment expression must be a variable or a property access.
tluaTableShorthandRemoved.tlua(13,4): error TLUA2454: Variable 'q' is used before being assigned.
tluaTableShorthandRemoved.tlua(18,2): error TLUA2364: The left-hand side of an assignment expression must be a variable or a property access.
tluaTableShorthandRemoved.tlua(24,14): error TLUA2454: Variable 'q' is used before being assigned.


==== tluaTableShorthandRemoved.tlua (5 errors) ====
    // TS object shorthand is gone: `{x}` is a positional entry `[1] = x`.
    local x = 1;
    local t = { x };
    local t1: number = t[1];
    // ... so it is not assignable where a property `x` is required.
    local s: { x: number } = { x };
                               ~
!!! error TLUA2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and '1' does not exist in type '{ x: number; }'.
    local es = {t1, s};
    
    // Destructuring assignment is gone with it: an object literal on the left of
    // `=` is an ordinary (erroneous) assignment target ...
    declare obj: { q: number; w?: number };
    local q: number;
    ({ q } = obj);
     ~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2364: The left-hand side of an assignment expression must be a variable or a property access.
       ~
!!! error TLUA2454: Variable 'q' is used before being assigned.
    
    // ... and `{ w = 5 }` has exactly one meaning -- a Lua keyed field -- so the
    // old cover-grammar destructuring reading is an error too.
    local w: number | nil;
    ({ w = 5 } = obj);
     ~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2364: The left-hand side of an assignment expression must be a variable or a property access.
    
    // Lua spells the extraction with member access ...
    local q3 = obj.q;
    // ... and parameter binding patterns are a separate grammar, untouched.
    function take({ q: q4 }: { q: number }): number { return q4; }
    local all = {q, w, q3, take};
                 ~
!!! error TLUA2454: Variable 'q' is used before being assigned.
    
    // `export {x}` is different syntax, untouched.
    { x };
